Overview

On this page **On this page**

* * *

This document applies to Crossplane v1.19 and not to the latest release v2.0.

![Crossplane Popsicle Truck](https://docs.crossplane.io/content/media/banner_hu_616f60d369258ad3.webp)

  

Crossplane is an open source Kubernetes extension that transforms your Kubernetes cluster into a **universal control plane**.

Crossplane lets you manage anything, anywhere, all through standard Kubernetes APIs. Crossplane can even let you [order a pizza](https://blog.crossplane.io/providers-101-ordering-pizza-with-kubernetes-and-crossplane/)
 directly from Kubernetes. If it has an API, Crossplane can connect to it.

With Crossplane, platform teams can create new abstractions and custom APIs with the full power of Kubernetes policies, namespaces, role based access controls and more. Crossplane brings all your non-Kubernetes resources under one roof.

Custom APIs, created by platform teams, allow security and compliance enforcement across resources or clouds, without exposing any complexity to the developers. A single API call can create multiple resources, in multiple clouds and use Kubernetes as the control plane for everything.

Tip

**What’s a control plane?**

Control planes create and manage the lifecycle of resources. Control planes constantly _check_ that the intended resources exist, _report_ when the intended state doesn’t match reality and _act_ to make things right.

Crossplane extends the Kubernetes control plane to be a **universal control plane** to check, report and act on any resource, anywhere.

Get started
===========

*   [Install Crossplane](https://docs.crossplane.io/v1.19/software/install/)
     in your Kubernetes cluster
*   Learn more about how Crossplane works in the [Crossplane introduction](https://docs.crossplane.io/v1.19/getting-started/introduction/)

     and start a conversation with a community of over 7,000 operators.

 project.

# API Reference · Crossplane v2.0

[v2.0\
\
Latest](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#)

[master](https://docs.crossplane.io/master/api/)
 [v2.0\
\
Latest](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/)
[v1.20](https://docs.crossplane.io/v1.20/api/)
 [v1.19](https://docs.crossplane.io/v1.19/api/)

API Reference
=============

Kind

Group/Version

[Expand All](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#)

[Collapse All](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#)

CompositeResourceDefinition (deprecated)

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1

Expand All

Collapse All

[YAML](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/crds/apiextensions.crossplane.io_compositeresourcedefinitions.yaml)

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1

A CompositeResourceDefinition defines the schema for a new custom Kubernetes API.

Read the Crossplane documentation for [more information about CustomResourceDefinitions](https://docs.crossplane.io/latest/concepts/composite-resource-definitions)
.

  

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec)

CompositeResourceDefinitionSpec specifies the desired state of the definition.

claimNames object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-claimNames)

ClaimNames specifies the names of an optional composite resource claim. When claim names are specified Crossplane will create a namespaced ‘composite resource claim’ CRD that corresponds to the defined composite resource. This composite resource claim acts as a namespaced proxy for the composite resource; creating, updating, or deleting the claim will create, update, or delete a corresponding composite resource. You may add claim names to an existing CompositeResourceDefinition, but they cannot be changed or removed once they have been set.

categories \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-claimNames-categories)

kind string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-claimNames-kind)

kind is the serialized kind of the resource. It is normally CamelCase and singular. Custom resource instances will use this value as the `kind` attribute in API calls.

listKind string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-claimNames-listKind)

listKind is the serialized kind of the list for this resource. Defaults to “`kind`List”.

plural string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-claimNames-plural)

plural is the plural name of the resource to serve. The custom resources are served under `/apis/<group>/<version>/.../<plural>`. Must match the name of the CustomResourceDefinition (in the form `<names.plural>.<group>`). Must be all lowercase.

shortNames \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-claimNames-shortNames)

singular string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-claimNames-singular)

singular is the singular name of the resource. It must be all lowercase. Defaults to lowercased `kind`.

connectionSecretKeys \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-connectionSecretKeys)

conversion object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-conversion)

Conversion defines all conversion settings for the defined Composite resource.

strategy string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-strategy)

strategy specifies how custom resources are converted between versions. Allowed values are:

*   `"None"`: The converter only change the apiVersion and would not touch any other field in the custom resource.
*   `"Webhook"`: API Server will call to an external webhook to do the conversion. Additional information is needed for this option. This requires spec.preserveUnknownFields to be false, and spec.conversion.webhook to be set.

webhook object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook)

webhook describes how to call the conversion webhook. Required when `strategy` is set to `"Webhook"`.

clientConfig object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig)

clientConfig is the instructions for how to call the webhook if strategy is `Webhook`.

caBundle string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-caBundle)

caBundle is a PEM encoded CA bundle which will be used to validate the webhook’s server certificate. If unspecified, system trust roots on the apiserver are used.

service object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-service)

service is a reference to the service for this webhook. Either service or url must be specified.

If the webhook is running within the cluster, then you should use `service`.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-service-name)

name is the name of the service. Required

namespace string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-service-namespace)

namespace is the namespace of the service. Required

path string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-service-path)

path is an optional URL path at which the webhook will be contacted.

port integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-service-port)

port is an optional service port at which the webhook will be contacted. `port` should be a valid port number (1-65535, inclusive). Defaults to 443 for backward compatibility.

url string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-url)

url gives the location of the webhook, in standard URL form (`scheme://host:port/path`). Exactly one of `url` or `service` must be specified.

The `host` should not refer to a service running in the cluster; use the `service` field instead. The host might be resolved via external DNS in some apiservers (e.g., `kube-apiserver` cannot resolve in-cluster DNS as that would be a layering violation). `host` may also be an IP address.

Please note that using `localhost` or `127.0.0.1` as a `host` is risky unless you take great care to run this webhook on all hosts which run an apiserver which might need to make calls to this webhook. Such installs are likely to be non-portable, i.e., not easy to turn up in a new cluster.

The scheme must be “https”; the URL must begin with “https://”.

A path is optional, and if present may be any string permissible in a URL. You may use the path to pass an arbitrary string to the webhook, for example, a cluster identifier.

Attempting to use a user or basic auth e.g. “user:password@” is not allowed. Fragments ("#…") and query parameters ("?…") are not allowed, either.

conversionReviewVersions \[ \]string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-conversionReviewVersions)

defaultCompositeDeletePolicy One of Background Foreground

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-defaultCompositeDeletePolicy)

_Default: `Background`_

DefaultCompositeDeletePolicy is the policy used when deleting the Composite that is associated with the Claim if no policy has been specified.

defaultCompositionRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-defaultCompositionRef)

DefaultCompositionRef refers to the Composition resource that will be used in case no composition selector is given.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-defaultCompositionRef-name)

Name of the Composition.

defaultCompositionUpdatePolicy One of Automatic Manual

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-defaultCompositionUpdatePolicy)

_Default: `Automatic`_

DefaultCompositionUpdatePolicy is the policy used when updating composites after a new Composition Revision has been created if no policy has been specified on the composite.

enforcedCompositionRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-enforcedCompositionRef)

EnforcedCompositionRef refers to the Composition resource that will be used by all composite instances whose schema is defined by this definition.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-enforcedCompositionRef-name)

Name of the Composition.

group string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-group)

Group specifies the API group of the defined composite resource. Composite resources are served under `/apis/<group>/...`. Must match the name of the XRD (in the form `<names.plural>.<group>`).

metadata object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-metadata)

Metadata specifies the desired metadata for the defined composite resource and claim CRD’s.

annotations object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-metadata-annotations)

Annotations is an unstructured key value map stored with a resource that may be set by external tools to store and retrieve arbitrary metadata. They are not queryable and should be preserved when modifying objects. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations)

labels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-metadata-labels)

Map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize (scope and select) objects. May match selectors of replication controllers More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels)
 and services. These labels are added to the composite resource and claim CRD’s in addition to any labels defined by `CompositionResourceDefinition` `metadata.labels`.

names object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-names)

Names specifies the resource and kind names of the defined composite resource.

categories \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-names-categories)

kind string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-names-kind)

kind is the serialized kind of the resource. It is normally CamelCase and singular. Custom resource instances will use this value as the `kind` attribute in API calls.

listKind string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-names-listKind)

listKind is the serialized kind of the list for this resource. Defaults to “`kind`List”.

plural string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-names-plural)

plural is the plural name of the resource to serve. The custom resources are served under `/apis/<group>/<version>/.../<plural>`. Must match the name of the CustomResourceDefinition (in the form `<names.plural>.<group>`). Must be all lowercase.

shortNames \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-names-shortNames)

singular string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-names-singular)

singular is the singular name of the resource. It must be all lowercase. Defaults to lowercased `kind`.

scope One of LegacyCluster Namespaced Cluster

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-scope)

_Default: `LegacyCluster`_

Scope of the defined composite resource. Namespaced composite resources are scoped to a single namespace. Cluster scoped composite resource exist outside the scope of any namespace. Neither can be claimed. Legacy cluster scoped composite resources are cluster scoped resources that can be claimed.

versions \[ \]object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions)

CompositeResourceDefinitionVersion describes a version of an XR.

additionalPrinterColumns \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns)

CustomResourceColumnDefinition specifies a column for server side printing.

description string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns-description)

description is a human readable description of this column.

format string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns-format)

format is an optional OpenAPI type definition for this column. The ’name’ format is applied to the primary identifier column to assist in clients identifying column is the resource name. See [https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#data-types](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#data-types)
 for details.

jsonPath string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns-jsonPath)

jsonPath is a simple JSON path (i.e. with array notation) which is evaluated against each custom resource to produce the value for this column.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns-name)

name is a human readable name for the column.

priority integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns-priority)

priority is an integer defining the relative importance of this column compared to others. Lower numbers are considered higher priority. Columns that may be omitted in limited space scenarios should be given a priority greater than 0.

type string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns-type)

type is an OpenAPI type definition for this column. See [https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#data-types](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#data-types)
 for details.

deprecated boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-deprecated)

The deprecated field specifies that this version is deprecated and should not be used.

deprecationWarning string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-deprecationWarning)

DeprecationWarning specifies the message that should be shown to the user when using this version.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-name)

Name of this version, e.g. “v1”, “v2beta1”, etc. Composite resources are served under this version at `/apis/<group>/<version>/...` if `served` is true.

referenceable boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-referenceable)

Referenceable specifies that this version may be referenced by a Composition in order to configure which resources an XR may be composed of. Exactly one version must be marked as referenceable; all Compositions must target only the referenceable version. The referenceable version must be served. It’s mapped to the CRD’s `spec.versions[*].storage` field.

schema object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-schema)

Schema describes the schema used for validation, pruning, and defaulting of this version of the defined composite resource. Fields required by all composite resources will be injected into this schema automatically, and will override equivalently named fields in this schema. Omitting this schema results in a schema that contains only the fields required by all composite resources.

openAPIV3Schema object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-schema-openAPIV3Schema)

OpenAPIV3Schema is the OpenAPI v3 schema to use for validation and pruning.

served boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-spec-versions-served)

Served specifies that this version should be served via REST APIs.

status object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status)

CompositeResourceDefinitionStatus shows the observed state of the definition.

conditions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-conditions)

A Condition that may apply to a resource.

lastTransitionTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-conditions-lastTransitionTime)

LastTransitionTime is the last time this condition transitioned from one status to another.

message string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-conditions-message)

A Message containing details about this condition’s last transition from one status to another, if any.

observedGeneration integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-conditions-observedGeneration)

ObservedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon. For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions\[x\].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-conditions-reason)

A Reason for this condition’s last transition from one status to another.

status string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-conditions-status)

Status of this condition; is it currently True, False, or Unknown?

type string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-conditions-type)

Type of this condition. At most one of each condition type may apply to a resource at any point in time.

controllers object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-controllers)

Controllers represents the status of the controllers that power this composite resource definition.

compositeResourceClaimType object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-controllers-compositeResourceClaimType)

The CompositeResourceClaimTypeRef is the type of composite resource claim that Crossplane is currently reconciling for this definition. Its version will eventually become consistent with the definition’s referenceable version. Note that clients may interact with any served type; this is simply the type that Crossplane interacts with.

apiVersion string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-controllers-compositeResourceClaimType-apiVersion)

APIVersion of the type.

kind string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-controllers-compositeResourceClaimType-kind)

Kind of the type.

compositeResourceType object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-controllers-compositeResourceType)

The CompositeResourceTypeRef is the type of composite resource that Crossplane is currently reconciling for this definition. Its version will eventually become consistent with the definition’s referenceable version. Note that clients may interact with any served type; this is simply the type that Crossplane interacts with.

apiVersion string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-controllers-compositeResourceType-apiVersion)

APIVersion of the type.

kind string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositeResourceDefinition-status-controllers-compositeResourceType-kind)

Kind of the type.

[Back to top](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#content)

CompositionRevision

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1

Expand All

Collapse All

[YAML](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/crds/apiextensions.crossplane.io_compositionrevisions.yaml)

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1

A CompositionRevision represents a revision of a Composition. Crossplane creates new revisions when there are changes to the Composition.

Crossplane creates and manages CompositionRevisions. Don’t directly edit CompositionRevisions.

  

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec)

CompositionRevisionSpec specifies the desired state of the composition revision.

compositeTypeRef object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-compositeTypeRef)

CompositeTypeRef specifies the type of composite resource that this composition is compatible with.

apiVersion string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-compositeTypeRef-apiVersion)

APIVersion of the type.

kind string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-compositeTypeRef-kind)

Kind of the type.

mode One of Pipeline

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-mode)

_Default: `Pipeline`_

Mode controls what type or “mode” of Composition will be used.

“Pipeline” indicates that a Composition specifies a pipeline of functions, each of which is responsible for producing composed resources that Crossplane should create or update.

pipeline \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline)

A PipelineStep in a function pipeline.

credentials \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-credentials)

FunctionCredentials are optional credentials that a function needs to run.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-credentials-name)

Name of this set of credentials.

secretRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef)

A SecretRef is a reference to a secret containing credentials that should be supplied to the function.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef-name)

Name of the secret.

namespace string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef-namespace)

Namespace of the secret.

source One of None Secret

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-credentials-source)

Source of the function credentials.

functionRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-functionRef)

FunctionRef is a reference to the function this step should execute.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-functionRef-name)

Name of the referenced Function.

input object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-input)

Input is an optional, arbitrary Kubernetes resource (i.e. a resource with an apiVersion and kind) that will be passed to the function as the ‘input’ of its RunFunctionRequest.

requirements object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-requirements)

Requirements are resource requirements that will be satisfied before this pipeline step is called for the first time. This allows pre-populating required resources without requiring a function to request them first.

requiredResources \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources)

RequiredResourceSelector selects a required resource.

apiVersion string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-apiVersion)

APIVersion of the required resource.

kind string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-kind)

Kind of the required resource.

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-matchLabels)

MatchLabels specifies the set of labels to match for finding the required resource. When specified, Name is ignored.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-name)

Name of the required resource.

namespace string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-namespace)

Namespace of the required resource if it is namespaced.

requirementName string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-requirementName)

RequirementName is the unique name to identify this required resource in the Required Resources map in the function request.

step string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-pipeline-step)

Step name. Must be unique within its Pipeline.

revision integer required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-revision)

Revision number. Newer revisions have larger numbers.

This number can change. When a Composition transitions from state A -> B -> A there will be only two CompositionRevisions. Crossplane will edit the original CompositionRevision to change its revision number from 0 to 2.

writeConnectionSecretsToNamespace string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-spec-writeConnectionSecretsToNamespace)

WriteConnectionSecretsToNamespace specifies the namespace in which the connection secrets of composite resource dynamically provisioned using this composition will be created.

status object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-status)

CompositionRevisionStatus shows the observed state of the composition revision.

conditions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-status-conditions)

A Condition that may apply to a resource.

lastTransitionTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-status-conditions-lastTransitionTime)

LastTransitionTime is the last time this condition transitioned from one status to another.

message string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-status-conditions-message)

A Message containing details about this condition’s last transition from one status to another, if any.

observedGeneration integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-status-conditions-observedGeneration)

ObservedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon. For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions\[x\].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-status-conditions-reason)

A Reason for this condition’s last transition from one status to another.

status string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-status-conditions-status)

Status of this condition; is it currently True, False, or Unknown?

type string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CompositionRevision-status-conditions-type)

Type of this condition. At most one of each condition type may apply to a resource at any point in time.

Composition

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1

Expand All

Collapse All

[YAML](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/crds/apiextensions.crossplane.io_compositions.yaml)

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1

A Composition defines a collection of managed resources or functions that Crossplane uses to create and manage new composite resources.

Read the Crossplane documentation for [more information about Compositions](https://docs.crossplane.io/latest/concepts/compositions)
.

  

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec)

CompositionSpec specifies desired state of a composition.

compositeTypeRef object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-compositeTypeRef)

CompositeTypeRef specifies the type of composite resource that this composition is compatible with.

apiVersion string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-compositeTypeRef-apiVersion)

APIVersion of the type.

kind string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-compositeTypeRef-kind)

Kind of the type.

mode One of Pipeline

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-mode)

_Default: `Pipeline`_

Mode controls what type or “mode” of Composition will be used.

“Pipeline” indicates that a Composition specifies a pipeline of functions, each of which is responsible for producing composed resources that Crossplane should create or update.

pipeline \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline)

A PipelineStep in a function pipeline.

credentials \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-credentials)

FunctionCredentials are optional credentials that a function needs to run.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-credentials-name)

Name of this set of credentials.

secretRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef)

A SecretRef is a reference to a secret containing credentials that should be supplied to the function.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef-name)

Name of the secret.

namespace string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef-namespace)

Namespace of the secret.

source One of None Secret

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-credentials-source)

Source of the function credentials.

functionRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-functionRef)

FunctionRef is a reference to the function this step should execute.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-functionRef-name)

Name of the referenced Function.

input object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-input)

Input is an optional, arbitrary Kubernetes resource (i.e. a resource with an apiVersion and kind) that will be passed to the function as the ‘input’ of its RunFunctionRequest.

requirements object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-requirements)

Requirements are resource requirements that will be satisfied before this pipeline step is called for the first time. This allows pre-populating required resources without requiring a function to request them first.

requiredResources \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources)

RequiredResourceSelector selects a required resource.

apiVersion string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-apiVersion)

APIVersion of the required resource.

kind string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-kind)

Kind of the required resource.

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-matchLabels)

MatchLabels specifies the set of labels to match for finding the required resource. When specified, Name is ignored.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-name)

Name of the required resource.

namespace string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-namespace)

Namespace of the required resource if it is namespaced.

requirementName string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-requirementName)

RequirementName is the unique name to identify this required resource in the Required Resources map in the function request.

step string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-pipeline-step)

Step name. Must be unique within its Pipeline.

writeConnectionSecretsToNamespace string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Composition-spec-writeConnectionSecretsToNamespace)

WriteConnectionSecretsToNamespace specifies the namespace in which the connection secrets of composite resource dynamically provisioned using this composition will be created.

EnvironmentConfig

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1beta1

[YAML](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/crds/apiextensions.crossplane.io_environmentconfigs.yaml)

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1beta1

An EnvironmentConfig contains user-defined unstructured values for use in a Composition.

Read the Crossplane documentation for [more information about EnvironmentConfigs](https://docs.crossplane.io/latest/concepts/environment-configs)
.

  

data object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#EnvironmentConfig-data)

The data of this EnvironmentConfig. This may contain any kind of structure that can be serialized into JSON.

ManagedResourceActivationPolicy

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1alpha1

Expand All

Collapse All

[YAML](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/crds/apiextensions.crossplane.io_managedresourceactivationpolicies.yaml)

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1alpha1

A ManagedResourceActivationPolicy defines the activation policy for ManagedResourceDefinitions.  

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceActivationPolicy-spec)

ManagedResourceActivationPolicySpec specifies the desired activation state of ManagedResourceDefinitions.

activate \[ \]string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceActivationPolicy-spec-activate)

ActivationPolicy matches on MRD names with wildcard prefix support.

status object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceActivationPolicy-status)

ManagedResourceActivationPolicyStatus shows the observed state of the policy.

activated \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceActivationPolicy-status-activated)

conditions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceActivationPolicy-status-conditions)

A Condition that may apply to a resource.

lastTransitionTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceActivationPolicy-status-conditions-lastTransitionTime)

LastTransitionTime is the last time this condition transitioned from one status to another.

message string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceActivationPolicy-status-conditions-message)

A Message containing details about this condition’s last transition from one status to another, if any.

observedGeneration integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceActivationPolicy-status-conditions-observedGeneration)

ObservedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon. For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions\[x\].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceActivationPolicy-status-conditions-reason)

A Reason for this condition’s last transition from one status to another.

status string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceActivationPolicy-status-conditions-status)

Status of this condition; is it currently True, False, or Unknown?

type string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceActivationPolicy-status-conditions-type)

Type of this condition. At most one of each condition type may apply to a resource at any point in time.

ManagedResourceDefinition

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1alpha1

Expand All

Collapse All

[YAML](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/crds/apiextensions.crossplane.io_managedresourcedefinitions.yaml)

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1alpha1

A ManagedResourceDefinition defines the schema for a new custom Kubernetes API.  

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec)

ManagedResourceDefinitionSpec specifies the desired state of the resource definition.

connectionDetails \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-connectionDetails)

ConnectionDetail holds keys and descriptions of connection secrets.

description string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-connectionDetails-description)

Description of how the key is used.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-connectionDetails-name)

Name of the key.

conversion object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-conversion)

Conversion defines conversion settings for the CRD.

strategy string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-strategy)

strategy specifies how custom resources are converted between versions. Allowed values are:

*   `"None"`: The converter only change the apiVersion and would not touch any other field in the custom resource.
*   `"Webhook"`: API Server will call to an external webhook to do the conversion. Additional information is needed for this option. This requires spec.preserveUnknownFields to be false, and spec.conversion.webhook to be set.

webhook object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook)

webhook describes how to call the conversion webhook. Required when `strategy` is set to `"Webhook"`.

clientConfig object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig)

clientConfig is the instructions for how to call the webhook if strategy is `Webhook`.

caBundle string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-caBundle)

caBundle is a PEM encoded CA bundle which will be used to validate the webhook’s server certificate. If unspecified, system trust roots on the apiserver are used.

service object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-service)

service is a reference to the service for this webhook. Either service or url must be specified.

If the webhook is running within the cluster, then you should use `service`.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-service-name)

name is the name of the service. Required

namespace string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-service-namespace)

namespace is the namespace of the service. Required

path string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-service-path)

path is an optional URL path at which the webhook will be contacted.

port integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-service-port)

port is an optional service port at which the webhook will be contacted. `port` should be a valid port number (1-65535, inclusive). Defaults to 443 for backward compatibility.

url string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-clientConfig-url)

url gives the location of the webhook, in standard URL form (`scheme://host:port/path`). Exactly one of `url` or `service` must be specified.

The `host` should not refer to a service running in the cluster; use the `service` field instead. The host might be resolved via external DNS in some apiservers (e.g., `kube-apiserver` cannot resolve in-cluster DNS as that would be a layering violation). `host` may also be an IP address.

Please note that using `localhost` or `127.0.0.1` as a `host` is risky unless you take great care to run this webhook on all hosts which run an apiserver which might need to make calls to this webhook. Such installs are likely to be non-portable, i.e., not easy to turn up in a new cluster.

The scheme must be “https”; the URL must begin with “https://”.

A path is optional, and if present may be any string permissible in a URL. You may use the path to pass an arbitrary string to the webhook, for example, a cluster identifier.

Attempting to use a user or basic auth e.g. “user:password@” is not allowed. Fragments ("#…") and query parameters ("?…") are not allowed, either.

conversionReviewVersions \[ \]string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-conversion-webhook-conversionReviewVersions)

group string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-group)

Group is the API group of the defined custom resource. The custom resources are served under `/apis/<group>/...`. Must match the name of the CustomResourceDefinition (in the form `<names.plural>.<group>`).

names object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-names)

Names specify the resource and kind names for the custom resource.

categories \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-names-categories)

kind string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-names-kind)

kind is the serialized kind of the resource. It is normally CamelCase and singular. Custom resource instances will use this value as the `kind` attribute in API calls.

listKind string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-names-listKind)

listKind is the serialized kind of the list for this resource. Defaults to “`kind`List”.

plural string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-names-plural)

plural is the plural name of the resource to serve. The custom resources are served under `/apis/<group>/<version>/.../<plural>`. Must match the name of the CustomResourceDefinition (in the form `<names.plural>.<group>`). Must be all lowercase.

shortNames \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-names-shortNames)

singular string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-names-singular)

singular is the singular name of the resource. It must be all lowercase. Defaults to lowercased `kind`.

preserveUnknownFields boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-preserveUnknownFields)

PreserveUnknownFields indicates that object fields which are not specified in the OpenAPI schema should be preserved when persisting to storage. apiVersion, kind, metadata and known fields inside metadata are always preserved. This field is deprecated in favor of setting `x-preserve-unknown-fields` to true in `spec.versions[*].schema.openAPIV3Schema`. See [https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/extend-kubernetes/custom-resources/custom-resource-definitions/#field-pruning](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/extend-kubernetes/custom-resources/custom-resource-definitions/#field-pruning)
 for details.

scope One of Namespaced Cluster required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-scope)

_Default: `Namespaced`_

Scope indicates whether the defined custom resource is cluster- or namespace-scoped. Allowed values are `Cluster` and `Namespaced`.

state One of Active Inactive

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-state)

_Default: `Inactive`_

State toggles whether the underlying CRD is created or not.

versions \[ \]object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions)

CustomResourceDefinitionVersion describes a version for CRD.

additionalPrinterColumns \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns)

CustomResourceColumnDefinition specifies a column for server side printing.

description string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns-description)

description is a human readable description of this column.

format string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns-format)

format is an optional OpenAPI type definition for this column. The ’name’ format is applied to the primary identifier column to assist in clients identifying column is the resource name. See [https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#data-types](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#data-types)
 for details.

jsonPath string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns-jsonPath)

jsonPath is a simple JSON path (i.e. with array notation) which is evaluated against each custom resource to produce the value for this column.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns-name)

name is a human readable name for the column.

priority integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns-priority)

priority is an integer defining the relative importance of this column compared to others. Lower numbers are considered higher priority. Columns that may be omitted in limited space scenarios should be given a priority greater than 0.

type string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-additionalPrinterColumns-type)

type is an OpenAPI type definition for this column. See [https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#data-types](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#data-types)
 for details.

deprecated boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-deprecated)

Deprecated indicates this version of the custom resource API is deprecated. When set to true, API requests to this version receive a warning header in the server response. Defaults to false.

deprecationWarning string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-deprecationWarning)

DeprecationWarning overrides the default warning returned to API clients. May only be set when `deprecated` is true. The default warning indicates this version is deprecated and recommends use of the newest served version of equal or greater stability, if one exists.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-name)

Name is the version name, e.g. “v1”, “v2beta1”, etc. The custom resources are served under this version at `/apis/<group>/<version>/...` if `served` is true.

schema object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-schema)

Schema describes the schema used for validation, pruning, and defaulting of this version of the custom resource.

openAPIV3Schema object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-schema-openAPIV3Schema)

OpenAPIV3Schema is the OpenAPI v3 schema to use for validation and pruning.

selectableFields \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-selectableFields)

SelectableField specifies the JSON path of a field that may be used with field selectors.

jsonPath string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-selectableFields-jsonPath)

jsonPath is a simple JSON path which is evaluated against each custom resource to produce a field selector value. Only JSON paths without the array notation are allowed. Must point to a field of type string, boolean or integer. Types with enum values and strings with formats are allowed. If jsonPath refers to absent field in a resource, the jsonPath evaluates to an empty string. Must not point to metdata fields. Required.

served boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-served)

Served is a flag enabling/disabling this version from being served via REST APIs

storage boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-storage)

Storage indicates this version should be used when persisting custom resources to storage. There must be exactly one version with storage=true.

subresources object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-subresources)

Subresources specify what subresources this version of the defined custom resource have.

scale object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-subresources-scale)

scale indicates the custom resource should serve a `/scale` subresource that returns an `autoscaling/v1` Scale object.

labelSelectorPath string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-subresources-scale-labelSelectorPath)

labelSelectorPath defines the JSON path inside of a custom resource that corresponds to Scale `status.selector`. Only JSON paths without the array notation are allowed. Must be a JSON Path under `.status` or `.spec`. Must be set to work with HorizontalPodAutoscaler. The field pointed by this JSON path must be a string field (not a complex selector struct) which contains a serialized label selector in string form. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/access-kubernetes-api/custom-resources/custom-resource-definitions#scale-subresource](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/access-kubernetes-api/custom-resources/custom-resource-definitions#scale-subresource)
 If there is no value under the given path in the custom resource, the `status.selector` value in the `/scale` subresource will default to the empty string.

specReplicasPath string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-subresources-scale-specReplicasPath)

specReplicasPath defines the JSON path inside of a custom resource that corresponds to Scale `spec.replicas`. Only JSON paths without the array notation are allowed. Must be a JSON Path under `.spec`. If there is no value under the given path in the custom resource, the `/scale` subresource will return an error on GET.

statusReplicasPath string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-subresources-scale-statusReplicasPath)

statusReplicasPath defines the JSON path inside of a custom resource that corresponds to Scale `status.replicas`. Only JSON paths without the array notation are allowed. Must be a JSON Path under `.status`. If there is no value under the given path in the custom resource, the `status.replicas` value in the `/scale` subresource will default to 0.

status object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-spec-versions-subresources-status)

status indicates the custom resource should serve a `/status` subresource. When enabled:

1.  requests to the custom resource primary endpoint ignore changes to the `status` stanza of the object.
2.  requests to the custom resource `/status` subresource ignore changes to anything other than the `status` stanza of the object.

status object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-status)

ManagedResourceDefinitionStatus shows the observed state of the resource definition.

conditions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-status-conditions)

A Condition that may apply to a resource.

lastTransitionTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-status-conditions-lastTransitionTime)

LastTransitionTime is the last time this condition transitioned from one status to another.

message string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-status-conditions-message)

A Message containing details about this condition’s last transition from one status to another, if any.

observedGeneration integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-status-conditions-observedGeneration)

ObservedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon. For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions\[x\].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-status-conditions-reason)

A Reason for this condition’s last transition from one status to another.

status string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-status-conditions-status)

Status of this condition; is it currently True, False, or Unknown?

type string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ManagedResourceDefinition-status-conditions-type)

Type of this condition. At most one of each condition type may apply to a resource at any point in time.

Usage (deprecated)

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1alpha1

Expand All

Collapse All

[YAML](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/crds/apiextensions.crossplane.io_usages.yaml)

apiextensions.crossplane.io/v1alpha1

A Usage defines a deletion blocking relationship between two resources.

Usages prevent accidental deletion of a single resource or deletion of resources with dependent resources.

Read the Crossplane documentation for [more information about Usages](https://docs.crossplane.io/latest/concepts/usages)
.

Deprecated: Use protection.crossplane.io Usage or ClusterUsage.

  

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec)

UsageSpec defines the desired state of Usage.

by object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-by)

By is the resource that is “using the other resource”.

apiVersion string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-by-apiVersion)

API version of the referent.

kind string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-by-kind)

Kind of the referent. More info: [https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds)

resourceRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-by-resourceRef)

Reference to the resource.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-by-resourceRef-name)

Name of the referent.

resourceSelector object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-by-resourceSelector)

Selector to the resource. This field will be ignored if ResourceRef is set.

matchControllerRef boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-by-resourceSelector-matchControllerRef)

MatchControllerRef ensures an object with the same controller reference as the selecting object is selected.

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-by-resourceSelector-matchLabels)

MatchLabels ensures an object with matching labels is selected.

of object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-of)

Of is the resource that is “being used”.

apiVersion string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-of-apiVersion)

API version of the referent.

kind string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-of-kind)

Kind of the referent. More info: [https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds)

resourceRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-of-resourceRef)

Reference to the resource.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-of-resourceRef-name)

Name of the referent.

resourceSelector object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-of-resourceSelector)

Selector to the resource. This field will be ignored if ResourceRef is set.

matchControllerRef boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-of-resourceSelector-matchControllerRef)

MatchControllerRef ensures an object with the same controller reference as the selecting object is selected.

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-of-resourceSelector-matchLabels)

MatchLabels ensures an object with matching labels is selected.

reason string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-reason)

Reason is the reason for blocking deletion of the resource.

replayDeletion boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-spec-replayDeletion)

ReplayDeletion will trigger a deletion on the used resource during the deletion of the usage itself, if it was attempted to be deleted at least once.

status object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-status)

UsageStatus defines the observed state of Usage.

conditions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-status-conditions)

A Condition that may apply to a resource.

lastTransitionTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-status-conditions-lastTransitionTime)

LastTransitionTime is the last time this condition transitioned from one status to another.

message string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-status-conditions-message)

A Message containing details about this condition’s last transition from one status to another, if any.

observedGeneration integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-status-conditions-observedGeneration)

ObservedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon. For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions\[x\].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-status-conditions-reason)

A Reason for this condition’s last transition from one status to another.

status string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-status-conditions-status)

Status of this condition; is it currently True, False, or Unknown?

type string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Usage-status-conditions-type)

Type of this condition. At most one of each condition type may apply to a resource at any point in time.

CronOperation

ops.crossplane.io/v1alpha1

Expand All

Collapse All

[YAML](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/crds/ops.crossplane.io_cronoperations.yaml)

ops.crossplane.io/v1alpha1

A CronOperation creates Operations on a cron schedule.  

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec)

CronOperationSpec specifies the desired state of a CronOperation.

concurrencyPolicy One of Allow Forbid Replace

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-concurrencyPolicy)

_Default: `Allow`_

ConcurrencyPolicy specifies how to treat concurrent executions of an operation.

failedHistoryLimit integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-failedHistoryLimit)

_Default: `1`_

FailedHistoryLimit is the number of failed Operations to retain.

operationTemplate object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate)

OperationTemplate is the template for the Operation to be created.

metadata object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-metadata)

Standard object metadata.

spec object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec)

Spec is the specification of the Operation to be created.

mode One of Pipeline required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-mode)

_Default: `Pipeline`_

Mode controls what type or “mode” of operation will be used.

“Pipeline” indicates that an Operation specifies a pipeline of functions, each of which is responsible for implementing its logic.

pipeline \[ \]object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline)

A PipelineStep in an operation function pipeline.

credentials \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-credentials)

FunctionCredentials are optional credentials that a function needs to run.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-credentials-name)

Name of this set of credentials.

secretRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef)

A SecretRef is a reference to a secret containing credentials that should be supplied to the function.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef-name)

Name of the secret.

namespace string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef-namespace)

Namespace of the secret.

source One of None Secret

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-credentials-source)

Source of the function credentials.

functionRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-functionRef)

FunctionRef is a reference to the function this step should execute.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-functionRef-name)

Name of the referenced function.

input object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-input)

Input is an optional, arbitrary Kubernetes resource (i.e. a resource with an apiVersion and kind) that will be passed to the unction as the ‘input’ of its RunFunctionRequest.

requirements object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements)

Requirements are resource requirements that will be satisfied before this pipeline step is called for the first time. This allows pre-populating required resources without requiring a function to request them first.

requiredResources \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources)

RequiredResourceSelector selects resources that should be fetched before a pipeline step runs.

apiVersion string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-apiVersion)

APIVersion of resources to select.

kind string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-kind)

Kind of resources to select.

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-matchLabels)

MatchLabels matches resources by label selector. Only one of Name or MatchLabels may be specified.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-name)

Name matches a single resource by name. Only one of Name or MatchLabels may be specified.

namespace string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-namespace)

Namespace to search for resources. Optional for cluster-scoped resources.

requirementName string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-requirementName)

RequirementName uniquely identifies this group of resources. This name will be used as the key in RunFunctionRequest.required\_resources.

step string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-step)

Step name. Must be unique within its Pipeline.

retryLimit integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-retryLimit)

RetryLimit configures how many times the operation may fail. When the failure limit is exceeded, the operation will not be retried.

schedule string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-schedule)

Schedule is the cron schedule for the operation.

startingDeadlineSeconds integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-startingDeadlineSeconds)

StartingDeadlineSeconds is the deadline in seconds for starting the operation if it misses its scheduled time for any reason.

successfulHistoryLimit integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-spec-successfulHistoryLimit)

_Default: `3`_

SuccessfulHistoryLimit is the number of successful Operations to retain.

status object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-status)

CronOperationStatus represents the observed state of a CronOperation.

conditions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-status-conditions)

A Condition that may apply to a resource.

lastTransitionTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-status-conditions-lastTransitionTime)

LastTransitionTime is the last time this condition transitioned from one status to another.

message string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-status-conditions-message)

A Message containing details about this condition’s last transition from one status to another, if any.

observedGeneration integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-status-conditions-observedGeneration)

ObservedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon. For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions\[x\].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-status-conditions-reason)

A Reason for this condition’s last transition from one status to another.

status string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-status-conditions-status)

Status of this condition; is it currently True, False, or Unknown?

type string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-status-conditions-type)

Type of this condition. At most one of each condition type may apply to a resource at any point in time.

lastScheduleTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-status-lastScheduleTime)

LastScheduleTime is the last time the CronOperation was scheduled.

lastSuccessfulTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-status-lastSuccessfulTime)

LastSuccessfulTime is the last time the CronOperation was successfully completed.

runningOperationRefs \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-status-runningOperationRefs)

A RunningOperationRef is a reference to a running operation.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#CronOperation-status-runningOperationRefs-name)

Name of the active operation.

Operation

ops.crossplane.io/v1alpha1

Expand All

Collapse All

[YAML](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/crds/ops.crossplane.io_operations.yaml)

ops.crossplane.io/v1alpha1

An Operation defines a pipeline of functions that together constitute a day two operation.  

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec)

OperationSpec specifies desired state of an operation.

mode One of Pipeline required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-mode)

_Default: `Pipeline`_

Mode controls what type or “mode” of operation will be used.

“Pipeline” indicates that an Operation specifies a pipeline of functions, each of which is responsible for implementing its logic.

pipeline \[ \]object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline)

A PipelineStep in an operation function pipeline.

credentials \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-credentials)

FunctionCredentials are optional credentials that a function needs to run.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-credentials-name)

Name of this set of credentials.

secretRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef)

A SecretRef is a reference to a secret containing credentials that should be supplied to the function.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef-name)

Name of the secret.

namespace string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef-namespace)

Namespace of the secret.

source One of None Secret

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-credentials-source)

Source of the function credentials.

functionRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-functionRef)

FunctionRef is a reference to the function this step should execute.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-functionRef-name)

Name of the referenced function.

input object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-input)

Input is an optional, arbitrary Kubernetes resource (i.e. a resource with an apiVersion and kind) that will be passed to the unction as the ‘input’ of its RunFunctionRequest.

requirements object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-requirements)

Requirements are resource requirements that will be satisfied before this pipeline step is called for the first time. This allows pre-populating required resources without requiring a function to request them first.

requiredResources \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources)

RequiredResourceSelector selects resources that should be fetched before a pipeline step runs.

apiVersion string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-apiVersion)

APIVersion of resources to select.

kind string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-kind)

Kind of resources to select.

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-matchLabels)

MatchLabels matches resources by label selector. Only one of Name or MatchLabels may be specified.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-name)

Name matches a single resource by name. Only one of Name or MatchLabels may be specified.

namespace string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-namespace)

Namespace to search for resources. Optional for cluster-scoped resources.

requirementName string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-requirementName)

RequirementName uniquely identifies this group of resources. This name will be used as the key in RunFunctionRequest.required\_resources.

step string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-pipeline-step)

Step name. Must be unique within its Pipeline.

retryLimit integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-spec-retryLimit)

RetryLimit configures how many times the operation may fail. When the failure limit is exceeded, the operation will not be retried.

status object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status)

OperationStatus represents the observed state of an operation.

appliedResourceRefs \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-appliedResourceRefs)

An AppliedResourceRef is a reference to a resource an Operation applied.

apiVersion string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-appliedResourceRefs-apiVersion)

APIVersion of the applied resource.

kind string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-appliedResourceRefs-kind)

Kind of the applied resource.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-appliedResourceRefs-name)

Name of the applied resource.

namespace string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-appliedResourceRefs-namespace)

Namespace of the applied resource.

conditions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-conditions)

A Condition that may apply to a resource.

lastTransitionTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-conditions-lastTransitionTime)

LastTransitionTime is the last time this condition transitioned from one status to another.

message string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-conditions-message)

A Message containing details about this condition’s last transition from one status to another, if any.

observedGeneration integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-conditions-observedGeneration)

ObservedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon. For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions\[x\].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-conditions-reason)

A Reason for this condition’s last transition from one status to another.

status string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-conditions-status)

Status of this condition; is it currently True, False, or Unknown?

type string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-conditions-type)

Type of this condition. At most one of each condition type may apply to a resource at any point in time.

failures integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-failures)

Number of operation failures.

pipeline \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-pipeline)

PipelineStepStatus represents the status of an individual pipeline step.

output object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-pipeline-output)

Output of this step.

step string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Operation-status-pipeline-step)

Step name. Unique within its Pipeline.
