WatchOperation

ops.crossplane.io/v1alpha1

Expand All

Collapse All

[YAML](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/crds/ops.crossplane.io_watchoperations.yaml)

ops.crossplane.io/v1alpha1

A WatchOperation creates Operations when watched resources change.  

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec)

WatchOperationSpec specifies the desired state of a WatchOperation.

concurrencyPolicy One of Allow Forbid Replace

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-concurrencyPolicy)

_Default: `Allow`_

ConcurrencyPolicy specifies how to treat concurrent executions of an operation.

failedHistoryLimit integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-failedHistoryLimit)

_Default: `1`_

FailedHistoryLimit is the number of failed Operations to retain.

operationTemplate object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate)

OperationTemplate is the template for the Operation to be created.

metadata object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-metadata)

Standard object metadata.

spec object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec)

Spec is the specification of the Operation to be created.

mode One of Pipeline required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-mode)

_Default: `Pipeline`_

Mode controls what type or “mode” of operation will be used.

“Pipeline” indicates that an Operation specifies a pipeline of functions, each of which is responsible for implementing its logic.

pipeline \[ \]object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline)

A PipelineStep in an operation function pipeline.

credentials \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-credentials)

FunctionCredentials are optional credentials that a function needs to run.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-credentials-name)

Name of this set of credentials.

secretRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef)

A SecretRef is a reference to a secret containing credentials that should be supplied to the function.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef-name)

Name of the secret.

namespace string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-credentials-secretRef-namespace)

Namespace of the secret.

source One of None Secret

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-credentials-source)

Source of the function credentials.

functionRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-functionRef)

FunctionRef is a reference to the function this step should execute.

name string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-functionRef-name)

Name of the referenced function.

input object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-input)

Input is an optional, arbitrary Kubernetes resource (i.e. a resource with an apiVersion and kind) that will be passed to the unction as the ‘input’ of its RunFunctionRequest.

requirements object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements)

Requirements are resource requirements that will be satisfied before this pipeline step is called for the first time. This allows pre-populating required resources without requiring a function to request them first.

requiredResources \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources)

RequiredResourceSelector selects resources that should be fetched before a pipeline step runs.

apiVersion string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-apiVersion)

APIVersion of resources to select.

kind string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-kind)

Kind of resources to select.

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-matchLabels)

MatchLabels matches resources by label selector. Only one of Name or MatchLabels may be specified.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-name)

Name matches a single resource by name. Only one of Name or MatchLabels may be specified.

namespace string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-namespace)

Namespace to search for resources. Optional for cluster-scoped resources.

requirementName string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-requirements-requiredResources-requirementName)

RequirementName uniquely identifies this group of resources. This name will be used as the key in RunFunctionRequest.required\_resources.

step string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-pipeline-step)

Step name. Must be unique within its Pipeline.

retryLimit integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-operationTemplate-spec-retryLimit)

RetryLimit configures how many times the operation may fail. When the failure limit is exceeded, the operation will not be retried.

successfulHistoryLimit integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-successfulHistoryLimit)

_Default: `3`_

SuccessfulHistoryLimit is the number of successful Operations to retain.

watch object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-watch)

Watch specifies the resource to watch.

apiVersion string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-watch-apiVersion)

APIVersion of the resource to watch.

kind string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-watch-kind)

Kind of the resource to watch.

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-watch-matchLabels)

MatchLabels selects resources by label. If empty, all resources of the specified kind are watched.

namespace string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-spec-watch-namespace)

Namespace selects resources in a specific namespace. If empty, all namespaces are watched. Only applicable for namespaced resources.

status object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-status)

WatchOperationStatus represents the observed state of a WatchOperation.

conditions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-status-conditions)

A Condition that may apply to a resource.

lastTransitionTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-status-conditions-lastTransitionTime)

LastTransitionTime is the last time this condition transitioned from one status to another.

message string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-status-conditions-message)

A Message containing details about this condition’s last transition from one status to another, if any.

observedGeneration integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-status-conditions-observedGeneration)

ObservedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon. For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions\[x\].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-status-conditions-reason)

A Reason for this condition’s last transition from one status to another.

status string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-status-conditions-status)

Status of this condition; is it currently True, False, or Unknown?

type string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-status-conditions-type)

Type of this condition. At most one of each condition type may apply to a resource at any point in time.

lastScheduleTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-status-lastScheduleTime)

LastScheduleTime is the last time the WatchOperation created an Operation.

lastSuccessfulTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-status-lastSuccessfulTime)

LastSuccessfulTime is the last time the WatchOperation successfully completed an Operation.

runningOperationRefs \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-status-runningOperationRefs)

A RunningOperationRef is a reference to a running operation.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-status-runningOperationRefs-name)

Name of the active operation.

watchingResources integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#WatchOperation-status-watchingResources)

WatchingResources is the number of resources this WatchOperation is currently watching.

ConfigurationRevision

pkg.crossplane.io/v1

Expand All

Collapse All

[YAML](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/crds/pkg.crossplane.io_configurationrevisions.yaml)

pkg.crossplane.io/v1

A ConfigurationRevision represents a revision of a Configuration. Crossplane creates new revisions when there are changes to a Configuration.

Crossplane creates and manages ConfigurationRevision. Don’t directly edit ConfigurationRevisions.

  

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-spec)

PackageRevisionSpec specifies the desired state of a PackageRevision.

commonLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-spec-commonLabels)

Map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize (scope and select) objects. May match selectors of replication controllers and services. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/)

desiredState string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-spec-desiredState)

DesiredState of the PackageRevision. Can be either Active or Inactive.

ignoreCrossplaneConstraints boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-spec-ignoreCrossplaneConstraints)

_Default: `false`_

IgnoreCrossplaneConstraints indicates to the package manager whether to honor Crossplane version constrains specified by the package. Default is false.

image string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-spec-image)

Package image used by install Pod to extract package contents.

packagePullPolicy string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-spec-packagePullPolicy)

_Default: `IfNotPresent`_

PackagePullPolicy defines the pull policy for the package. It is also applied to any images pulled for the package, such as a provider’s controller image. Default is IfNotPresent.

packagePullSecrets \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-spec-packagePullSecrets)

LocalObjectReference contains enough information to let you locate the referenced object inside the same namespace.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-spec-packagePullSecrets-name)

_Default:_

Name of the referent. This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are almost certainly wrong. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names)

revision integer required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-spec-revision)

Revision number. Indicates when the revision will be garbage collected based on the parent’s RevisionHistoryLimit.

skipDependencyResolution boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-spec-skipDependencyResolution)

_Default: `false`_

SkipDependencyResolution indicates to the package manager whether to skip resolving dependencies for a package. Setting this value to true may have unintended consequences. Default is false.

status object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status)

PackageRevisionStatus represents the observed state of a PackageRevision.

appliedImageConfigRefs \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-appliedImageConfigRefs)

ImageConfigRef is a reference to an image config that indicates how the referenced image config was used by the package manager.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-appliedImageConfigRefs-name)

Name is the name of the image config.

reason string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-appliedImageConfigRefs-reason)

Reason indicates what the image config was used for.

capabilities \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-capabilities)

conditions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-conditions)

A Condition that may apply to a resource.

lastTransitionTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-conditions-lastTransitionTime)

LastTransitionTime is the last time this condition transitioned from one status to another.

message string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-conditions-message)

A Message containing details about this condition’s last transition from one status to another, if any.

observedGeneration integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-conditions-observedGeneration)

ObservedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon. For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions\[x\].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-conditions-reason)

A Reason for this condition’s last transition from one status to another.

status string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-conditions-status)

Status of this condition; is it currently True, False, or Unknown?

type string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-conditions-type)

Type of this condition. At most one of each condition type may apply to a resource at any point in time.

foundDependencies integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-foundDependencies)

Dependency information.

installedDependencies integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-installedDependencies)

invalidDependencies integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-invalidDependencies)

objectRefs \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-objectRefs)

A TypedReference refers to an object by Name, Kind, and APIVersion. It is commonly used to reference cluster-scoped objects or objects where the namespace is already known.

apiVersion string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-objectRefs-apiVersion)

APIVersion of the referenced object.

kind string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-objectRefs-kind)

Kind of the referenced object.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-objectRefs-name)

Name of the referenced object.

uid string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-objectRefs-uid)

UID of the referenced object.

resolvedImage string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#ConfigurationRevision-status-resolvedImage)

ResolvedPackage is the name of the package that was installed. It may be different from spec.image if the package path was rewritten using an image config.

[Back to top](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#content)

Configuration

pkg.crossplane.io/v1

Expand All

Collapse All

[YAML](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/crds/pkg.crossplane.io_configurations.yaml)

pkg.crossplane.io/v1

A Configuration installs an OCI compatible Crossplane package, extending Crossplane with support for new kinds of CompositeResourceDefinitions and Compositions.

Read the Crossplane documentation for [more information about Configuration packages](https://docs.crossplane.io/latest/concepts/packages)
.

  

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-spec)

ConfigurationSpec specifies details about a request to install a configuration to Crossplane.

commonLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-spec-commonLabels)

Map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize (scope and select) objects. May match selectors of replication controllers and services. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/)

ignoreCrossplaneConstraints boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-spec-ignoreCrossplaneConstraints)

_Default: `false`_

IgnoreCrossplaneConstraints indicates to the package manager whether to honor Crossplane version constrains specified by the package. Default is false.

package string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-spec-package)

Package is the name of the package that is being requested. must be a fully qualified image name, including the registry, repository, and tag. for example, “registry.example.com/repo/package:tag”.

packagePullPolicy string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-spec-packagePullPolicy)

_Default: `IfNotPresent`_

PackagePullPolicy defines the pull policy for the package. Default is IfNotPresent.

packagePullSecrets \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-spec-packagePullSecrets)

LocalObjectReference contains enough information to let you locate the referenced object inside the same namespace.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-spec-packagePullSecrets-name)

_Default:_

Name of the referent. This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are almost certainly wrong. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names)

revisionActivationPolicy string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-spec-revisionActivationPolicy)

_Default: `Automatic`_

RevisionActivationPolicy specifies how the package controller should update from one revision to the next. Options are Automatic or Manual. Default is Automatic.

revisionHistoryLimit integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-spec-revisionHistoryLimit)

_Default: `1`_

RevisionHistoryLimit dictates how the package controller cleans up old inactive package revisions. Defaults to 1. Can be disabled by explicitly setting to 0.

skipDependencyResolution boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-spec-skipDependencyResolution)

_Default: `false`_

SkipDependencyResolution indicates to the package manager whether to skip resolving dependencies for a package. Setting this value to true may have unintended consequences. Default is false.

status object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status)

ConfigurationStatus represents the observed state of a Configuration.

appliedImageConfigRefs \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status-appliedImageConfigRefs)

ImageConfigRef is a reference to an image config that indicates how the referenced image config was used by the package manager.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status-appliedImageConfigRefs-name)

Name is the name of the image config.

reason string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status-appliedImageConfigRefs-reason)

Reason indicates what the image config was used for.

conditions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status-conditions)

A Condition that may apply to a resource.

lastTransitionTime string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status-conditions-lastTransitionTime)

LastTransitionTime is the last time this condition transitioned from one status to another.

message string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status-conditions-message)

A Message containing details about this condition’s last transition from one status to another, if any.

observedGeneration integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status-conditions-observedGeneration)

ObservedGeneration represents the .metadata.generation that the condition was set based upon. For instance, if .metadata.generation is currently 12, but the .status.conditions\[x\].observedGeneration is 9, the condition is out of date with respect to the current state of the instance.

reason string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status-conditions-reason)

A Reason for this condition’s last transition from one status to another.

status string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status-conditions-status)

Status of this condition; is it currently True, False, or Unknown?

type string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status-conditions-type)

Type of this condition. At most one of each condition type may apply to a resource at any point in time.

currentIdentifier string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status-currentIdentifier)

CurrentIdentifier is the most recent package source that was used to produce a revision. The package manager uses this field to determine whether to check for package updates for a given source when packagePullPolicy is set to IfNotPresent. Manually removing this field will cause the package manager to check that the current revision is correct for the given package source.

currentRevision string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status-currentRevision)

CurrentRevision is the name of the current package revision. It will reflect the most up to date revision, whether it has been activated or not.

resolvedPackage string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#Configuration-status-resolvedPackage)

ResolvedPackage is the name of the package that was used for version resolution. It may be different from spec.package if the package path was rewritten using an image config.

[Back to top](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#content)

DeploymentRuntimeConfig

pkg.crossplane.io/v1beta1

Expand All

Collapse All

[YAML](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/crds/pkg.crossplane.io_deploymentruntimeconfigs.yaml)

pkg.crossplane.io/v1beta1

The DeploymentRuntimeConfig provides settings for the Kubernetes Deployment of a Provider or composition function package.

Read the Crossplane documentation for [more information about DeploymentRuntimeConfigs](https://docs.crossplane.io/latest/concepts/providers/#runtime-configuration)
.

  

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec)

DeploymentRuntimeConfigSpec specifies the configuration for a packaged controller. Values provided will override package manager defaults. Labels and annotations are passed to both the controller Deployment and ServiceAccount.

deploymentTemplate object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate)

DeploymentTemplate is the template for the Deployment object.

metadata object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-metadata)

Metadata contains the configurable metadata fields for the Deployment.

annotations object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-metadata-annotations)

Annotations is an unstructured key value map stored with a resource that may be set by external tools to store and retrieve arbitrary metadata. They are not queryable and should be preserved when modifying objects. More info: http:https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/

labels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-metadata-labels)

Map of string keys and values that can be used to organize and categorize (scope and select) objects. Labels will be merged with internal labels used by crossplane, and labels with a crossplane.io key might be overwritten. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/)

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-metadata-name)

Name is the name of the object.

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec)

Spec contains the configurable spec fields for the Deployment object.

minReadySeconds integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-minReadySeconds)

Minimum number of seconds for which a newly created pod should be ready without any of its container crashing, for it to be considered available. Defaults to 0 (pod will be considered available as soon as it is ready)

paused boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-paused)

Indicates that the deployment is paused.

progressDeadlineSeconds integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-progressDeadlineSeconds)

The maximum time in seconds for a deployment to make progress before it is considered to be failed. The deployment controller will continue to process failed deployments and a condition with a ProgressDeadlineExceeded reason will be surfaced in the deployment status. Note that progress will not be estimated during the time a deployment is paused. Defaults to 600s.

replicas integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-replicas)

Number of desired pods. This is a pointer to distinguish between explicit zero and not specified. Defaults to 1.

revisionHistoryLimit integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-revisionHistoryLimit)

The number of old ReplicaSets to retain to allow rollback. This is a pointer to distinguish between explicit zero and not specified. Defaults to 10.

selector object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-selector)

Label selector for pods. Existing ReplicaSets whose pods are selected by this will be the ones affected by this deployment. It must match the pod template’s labels.

matchExpressions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-selector-matchExpressions)

A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

key string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-selector-matchExpressions-key)

key is the label key that the selector applies to.

operator string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-selector-matchExpressions-operator)

operator represents a key’s relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.

values \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-selector-matchExpressions-values)

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-selector-matchLabels)

matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is “key”, the operator is “In”, and the values array contains only “value”. The requirements are ANDed.

strategy object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-strategy)

The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones.

rollingUpdate object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-strategy-rollingUpdate)

Rolling update config params. Present only if DeploymentStrategyType = RollingUpdate.

maxSurge Any of integer string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-strategy-rollingUpdate-maxSurge)

The maximum number of pods that can be scheduled above the desired number of pods. Value can be an absolute number (ex: 5) or a percentage of desired pods (ex: 10%). This can not be 0 if MaxUnavailable is 0. Absolute number is calculated from percentage by rounding up. Defaults to 25%. Example: when this is set to 30%, the new ReplicaSet can be scaled up immediately when the rolling update starts, such that the total number of old and new pods do not exceed 130% of desired pods. Once old pods have been killed, new ReplicaSet can be scaled up further, ensuring that total number of pods running at any time during the update is at most 130% of desired pods.

maxUnavailable Any of integer string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-strategy-rollingUpdate-maxUnavailable)

The maximum number of pods that can be unavailable during the update. Value can be an absolute number (ex: 5) or a percentage of desired pods (ex: 10%). Absolute number is calculated from percentage by rounding down. This can not be 0 if MaxSurge is 0. Defaults to 25%. Example: when this is set to 30%, the old ReplicaSet can be scaled down to 70% of desired pods immediately when the rolling update starts. Once new pods are ready, old ReplicaSet can be scaled down further, followed by scaling up the new ReplicaSet, ensuring that the total number of pods available at all times during the update is at least 70% of desired pods.

type string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-strategy-type)

Type of deployment. Can be “Recreate” or “RollingUpdate”. Default is RollingUpdate.

template object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template)

Template describes the pods that will be created. The only allowed template.spec.restartPolicy value is “Always”.

metadata object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-metadata)

Standard object’s metadata. More info: [https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#metadata)

annotations object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-metadata-annotations)

finalizers \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-metadata-finalizers)

labels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-metadata-labels)

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-metadata-name)

namespace string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-metadata-namespace)

spec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec)

Specification of the desired behavior of the pod. More info: [https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#spec-and-status](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#spec-and-status)

activeDeadlineSeconds integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-activeDeadlineSeconds)

Optional duration in seconds the pod may be active on the node relative to StartTime before the system will actively try to mark it failed and kill associated containers. Value must be a positive integer.

affinity object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity)

If specified, the pod’s scheduling constraints

nodeAffinity object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity)

Describes node affinity scheduling rules for the pod.

preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution)

An empty preferred scheduling term matches all objects with implicit weight 0 (i.e. it’s a no-op). A null preferred scheduling term matches no objects (i.e. is also a no-op).

preference object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-preference)

A node selector term, associated with the corresponding weight.

matchExpressions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-preference-matchExpressions)

A node selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

key string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-preference-matchExpressions-key)

The label key that the selector applies to.

operator string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-preference-matchExpressions-operator)

Represents a key’s relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist. Gt, and Lt.

values \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-preference-matchExpressions-values)

matchFields \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-preference-matchFields)

A node selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

key string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-preference-matchFields-key)

The label key that the selector applies to.

operator string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-preference-matchFields-operator)

Represents a key’s relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist. Gt, and Lt.

values \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-preference-matchFields-values)

weight integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-weight)

Weight associated with matching the corresponding nodeSelectorTerm, in the range 1-100.

requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution)

If the affinity requirements specified by this field are not met at scheduling time, the pod will not be scheduled onto the node. If the affinity requirements specified by this field cease to be met at some point during pod execution (e.g. due to an update), the system may or may not try to eventually evict the pod from its node.

nodeSelectorTerms \[ \]object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-nodeSelectorTerms)

A null or empty node selector term matches no objects. The requirements of them are ANDed. The TopologySelectorTerm type implements a subset of the NodeSelectorTerm.

matchExpressions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-nodeSelectorTerms-matchExpressions)

A node selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

key string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-nodeSelectorTerms-matchExpressions-key)

The label key that the selector applies to.

operator string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-nodeSelectorTerms-matchExpressions-operator)

Represents a key’s relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist. Gt, and Lt.

values \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-nodeSelectorTerms-matchExpressions-values)

matchFields \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-nodeSelectorTerms-matchFields)

A node selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

key string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-nodeSelectorTerms-matchFields-key)

The label key that the selector applies to.

operator string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-nodeSelectorTerms-matchFields-operator)

Represents a key’s relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist. Gt, and Lt.

values \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-nodeAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-nodeSelectorTerms-matchFields-values)

podAffinity object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity)

Describes pod affinity scheduling rules (e.g. co-locate this pod in the same node, zone, etc. as some other pod(s)).

preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution)

The weights of all of the matched WeightedPodAffinityTerm fields are added per-node to find the most preferred node(s)

podAffinityTerm object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm)

Required. A pod affinity term, associated with the corresponding weight.

labelSelector object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-labelSelector)

A label query over a set of resources, in this case pods. If it’s null, this PodAffinityTerm matches with no Pods.

matchExpressions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-labelSelector-matchExpressions)

A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

key string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-labelSelector-matchExpressions-key)

key is the label key that the selector applies to.

operator string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-labelSelector-matchExpressions-operator)

operator represents a key’s relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.

values \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-labelSelector-matchExpressions-values)

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-labelSelector-matchLabels)

matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is “key”, the operator is “In”, and the values array contains only “value”. The requirements are ANDed.

matchLabelKeys \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-matchLabelKeys)

mismatchLabelKeys \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-mismatchLabelKeys)

namespaceSelector object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaceSelector)

A label query over the set of namespaces that the term applies to. The term is applied to the union of the namespaces selected by this field and the ones listed in the namespaces field. null selector and null or empty namespaces list means “this pod’s namespace”. An empty selector ({}) matches all namespaces.

matchExpressions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions)

A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

key string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions-key)

key is the label key that the selector applies to.

operator string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions-operator)

operator represents a key’s relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.

values \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions-values)

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaceSelector-matchLabels)

matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is “key”, the operator is “In”, and the values array contains only “value”. The requirements are ANDed.

namespaces \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaces)

topologyKey string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-topologyKey)

This pod should be co-located (affinity) or not co-located (anti-affinity) with the pods matching the labelSelector in the specified namespaces, where co-located is defined as running on a node whose value of the label with key topologyKey matches that of any node on which any of the selected pods is running. Empty topologyKey is not allowed.

weight integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-weight)

weight associated with matching the corresponding podAffinityTerm, in the range 1-100.

requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution)

Defines a set of pods (namely those matching the labelSelector relative to the given namespace(s)) that this pod should be co-located (affinity) or not co-located (anti-affinity) with, where co-located is defined as running on a node whose value of the label with key matches that of any node on which a pod of the set of pods is running

labelSelector object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-labelSelector)

A label query over a set of resources, in this case pods. If it’s null, this PodAffinityTerm matches with no Pods.

matchExpressions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-labelSelector-matchExpressions)

A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

key string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-labelSelector-matchExpressions-key)

key is the label key that the selector applies to.

operator string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-labelSelector-matchExpressions-operator)

operator represents a key’s relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.

values \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-labelSelector-matchExpressions-values)

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-labelSelector-matchLabels)

matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is “key”, the operator is “In”, and the values array contains only “value”. The requirements are ANDed.

matchLabelKeys \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-matchLabelKeys)

mismatchLabelKeys \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-mismatchLabelKeys)

namespaceSelector object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaceSelector)

A label query over the set of namespaces that the term applies to. The term is applied to the union of the namespaces selected by this field and the ones listed in the namespaces field. null selector and null or empty namespaces list means “this pod’s namespace”. An empty selector ({}) matches all namespaces.

matchExpressions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions)

A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

key string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions-key)

key is the label key that the selector applies to.

operator string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions-operator)

operator represents a key’s relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.

values \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions-values)

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaceSelector-matchLabels)

matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is “key”, the operator is “In”, and the values array contains only “value”. The requirements are ANDed.

namespaces \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaces)

topologyKey string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-topologyKey)

This pod should be co-located (affinity) or not co-located (anti-affinity) with the pods matching the labelSelector in the specified namespaces, where co-located is defined as running on a node whose value of the label with key topologyKey matches that of any node on which any of the selected pods is running. Empty topologyKey is not allowed.

podAntiAffinity object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity)

Describes pod anti-affinity scheduling rules (e.g. avoid putting this pod in the same node, zone, etc. as some other pod(s)).

preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution)

The weights of all of the matched WeightedPodAffinityTerm fields are added per-node to find the most preferred node(s)

podAffinityTerm object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm)

Required. A pod affinity term, associated with the corresponding weight.

labelSelector object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-labelSelector)

A label query over a set of resources, in this case pods. If it’s null, this PodAffinityTerm matches with no Pods.

matchExpressions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-labelSelector-matchExpressions)

A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

key string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-labelSelector-matchExpressions-key)

key is the label key that the selector applies to.

operator string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-labelSelector-matchExpressions-operator)

operator represents a key’s relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.

values \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-labelSelector-matchExpressions-values)

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-labelSelector-matchLabels)

matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is “key”, the operator is “In”, and the values array contains only “value”. The requirements are ANDed.

matchLabelKeys \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-matchLabelKeys)

mismatchLabelKeys \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-mismatchLabelKeys)

namespaceSelector object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaceSelector)

A label query over the set of namespaces that the term applies to. The term is applied to the union of the namespaces selected by this field and the ones listed in the namespaces field. null selector and null or empty namespaces list means “this pod’s namespace”. An empty selector ({}) matches all namespaces.

matchExpressions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions)

A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

key string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions-key)

key is the label key that the selector applies to.

operator string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions-operator)

operator represents a key’s relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.

values \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions-values)

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaceSelector-matchLabels)

matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is “key”, the operator is “In”, and the values array contains only “value”. The requirements are ANDed.

namespaces \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-namespaces)

topologyKey string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-podAffinityTerm-topologyKey)

This pod should be co-located (affinity) or not co-located (anti-affinity) with the pods matching the labelSelector in the specified namespaces, where co-located is defined as running on a node whose value of the label with key topologyKey matches that of any node on which any of the selected pods is running. Empty topologyKey is not allowed.

weight integer

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-weight)

weight associated with matching the corresponding podAffinityTerm, in the range 1-100.

requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution)

Defines a set of pods (namely those matching the labelSelector relative to the given namespace(s)) that this pod should be co-located (affinity) or not co-located (anti-affinity) with, where co-located is defined as running on a node whose value of the label with key matches that of any node on which a pod of the set of pods is running

labelSelector object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-labelSelector)

A label query over a set of resources, in this case pods. If it’s null, this PodAffinityTerm matches with no Pods.

matchExpressions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-labelSelector-matchExpressions)

A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

key string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-labelSelector-matchExpressions-key)

key is the label key that the selector applies to.

operator string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-labelSelector-matchExpressions-operator)

operator represents a key’s relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.

values \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-labelSelector-matchExpressions-values)

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-labelSelector-matchLabels)

matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is “key”, the operator is “In”, and the values array contains only “value”. The requirements are ANDed.

matchLabelKeys \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-matchLabelKeys)

mismatchLabelKeys \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-mismatchLabelKeys)

namespaceSelector object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaceSelector)

A label query over the set of namespaces that the term applies to. The term is applied to the union of the namespaces selected by this field and the ones listed in the namespaces field. null selector and null or empty namespaces list means “this pod’s namespace”. An empty selector ({}) matches all namespaces.

matchExpressions \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions)

A label selector requirement is a selector that contains values, a key, and an operator that relates the key and values.

key string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions-key)

key is the label key that the selector applies to.

operator string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions-operator)

operator represents a key’s relationship to a set of values. Valid operators are In, NotIn, Exists and DoesNotExist.

values \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaceSelector-matchExpressions-values)

matchLabels object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaceSelector-matchLabels)

matchLabels is a map of {key,value} pairs. A single {key,value} in the matchLabels map is equivalent to an element of matchExpressions, whose key field is “key”, the operator is “In”, and the values array contains only “value”. The requirements are ANDed.

namespaces \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-namespaces)

topologyKey string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-affinity-podAntiAffinity-requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution-topologyKey)

This pod should be co-located (affinity) or not co-located (anti-affinity) with the pods matching the labelSelector in the specified namespaces, where co-located is defined as running on a node whose value of the label with key topologyKey matches that of any node on which any of the selected pods is running. Empty topologyKey is not allowed.

automountServiceAccountToken boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-automountServiceAccountToken)

AutomountServiceAccountToken indicates whether a service account token should be automatically mounted.

containers \[ \]object required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers)

A single application container that you want to run within a pod.

args \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-args)

command \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-command)

env \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env)

EnvVar represents an environment variable present in a Container.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-name)

Name of the environment variable. Must be a C\_IDENTIFIER.

value string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-value)

Variable references $(VAR\_NAME) are expanded using the previously defined environment variables in the container and any service environment variables. If a variable cannot be resolved, the reference in the input string will be unchanged. Double $$ are reduced to a single $, which allows for escaping the $(VAR\_NAME) syntax: i.e. “$$(VAR\_NAME)” will produce the string literal “$(VAR\_NAME)”. Escaped references will never be expanded, regardless of whether the variable exists or not. Defaults to “”.

valueFrom object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom)

Source for the environment variable’s value. Cannot be used if value is not empty.

configMapKeyRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-configMapKeyRef)

Selects a key of a ConfigMap.

key string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-configMapKeyRef-key)

The key to select.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-configMapKeyRef-name)

_Default:_

Name of the referent. This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are almost certainly wrong. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names)

optional boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-configMapKeyRef-optional)

Specify whether the ConfigMap or its key must be defined

fieldRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-fieldRef)

Selects a field of the pod: supports metadata.name, metadata.namespace, `metadata.labels['<KEY>']`, `metadata.annotations['<KEY>']`, spec.nodeName, spec.serviceAccountName, status.hostIP, status.podIP, status.podIPs.

apiVersion string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-fieldRef-apiVersion)

Version of the schema the FieldPath is written in terms of, defaults to “v1”.

fieldPath string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-fieldRef-fieldPath)

Path of the field to select in the specified API version.

resourceFieldRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-resourceFieldRef)

Selects a resource of the container: only resources limits and requests (limits.cpu, limits.memory, limits.ephemeral-storage, requests.cpu, requests.memory and requests.ephemeral-storage) are currently supported.

containerName string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-resourceFieldRef-containerName)

Container name: required for volumes, optional for env vars

divisor Any of integer string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-resourceFieldRef-divisor)

Specifies the output format of the exposed resources, defaults to “1”

resource string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-resourceFieldRef-resource)

Required: resource to select

secretKeyRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-secretKeyRef)

Selects a key of a secret in the pod’s namespace

key string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-secretKeyRef-key)

The key of the secret to select from. Must be a valid secret key.

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-secretKeyRef-name)

_Default:_

Name of the referent. This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are almost certainly wrong. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names)

optional boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-env-valueFrom-secretKeyRef-optional)

Specify whether the Secret or its key must be defined

envFrom \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-envFrom)

EnvFromSource represents the source of a set of ConfigMaps or Secrets

configMapRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-envFrom-configMapRef)

The ConfigMap to select from

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-envFrom-configMapRef-name)

_Default:_

Name of the referent. This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are almost certainly wrong. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names)

optional boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-envFrom-configMapRef-optional)

Specify whether the ConfigMap must be defined

prefix string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-envFrom-prefix)

Optional text to prepend to the name of each environment variable. Must be a C\_IDENTIFIER.

secretRef object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-envFrom-secretRef)

The Secret to select from

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-envFrom-secretRef-name)

_Default:_

Name of the referent. This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are almost certainly wrong. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names)

optional boolean

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-envFrom-secretRef-optional)

Specify whether the Secret must be defined

image string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-image)

Container image name. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images)
 This field is optional to allow higher level config management to default or override container images in workload controllers like Deployments and StatefulSets.

imagePullPolicy string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-imagePullPolicy)

Image pull policy. One of Always, Never, IfNotPresent. Defaults to Always if :latest tag is specified, or IfNotPresent otherwise. Cannot be updated. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images#updating-images](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images#updating-images)

lifecycle object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-lifecycle)

Actions that the management system should take in response to container lifecycle events. Cannot be updated.

postStart object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-lifecycle-postStart)

PostStart is called immediately after a container is created. If the handler fails, the container is terminated and restarted according to its restart policy. Other management of the container blocks until the hook completes. More info: [https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/container-lifecycle-hooks/#container-hooks)

exec object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-lifecycle-postStart-exec)

Exec specifies a command to execute in the container.

command \[ \]string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-lifecycle-postStart-exec-command)

httpGet object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-lifecycle-postStart-httpGet)

HTTPGet specifies an HTTP GET request to perform.

host string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-lifecycle-postStart-httpGet-host)

Host name to connect to, defaults to the pod IP. You probably want to set “Host” in httpHeaders instead.

httpHeaders \[ \]object

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-lifecycle-postStart-httpGet-httpHeaders)

HTTPHeader describes a custom header to be used in HTTP probes

name string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-lifecycle-postStart-httpGet-httpHeaders-name)

The header field name. This will be canonicalized upon output, so case-variant names will be understood as the same header.

value string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-lifecycle-postStart-httpGet-httpHeaders-value)

The header field value

path string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-lifecycle-postStart-httpGet-path)

Path to access on the HTTP server.

port Any of integer string required

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-lifecycle-postStart-httpGet-port)

Name or number of the port to access on the container. Number must be in the range 1 to 65535. Name must be an IANA\_SVC\_NAME.

scheme string

[](https://docs.crossplane.io/v2.0/api/#DeploymentRuntimeConfig-spec-deploymentTemplate-spec-template-spec-containers-lifecycle-postStart-httpGet-scheme)

Scheme to use for connecting to the host. Defaults to HTTP.

sleep object
